steps.title: Enter the table in Desmos
steps.content: Put the table into Desmos as $x_1=[-1,0,1,2]$ and $y_1=[16,17,18,19]$. Then run a linear regression with $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=1$ and $b=17$, so the data fit the line $y=x+17$.

steps.title: Test for linearity: compute successive differences
steps.content: By hand, the outputs increase by $1$ each time: $17-16=1$, $18-17=1$, and $19-18=1$. Equal differences mean the function is linear, and the positive difference means it is increasing.

steps.title: Determine direction and eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
Since the outputs increase as x increases, the function is increasing. Check each choice:
  • A: Decreasing linear — the function increases, not decreases. Eliminate A.
  • B: Increasing linear — constant positive differences confirm linear growth. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: Decreasing exponential — exponential functions have constant ratios, not differences, and this function increases. Eliminate C.
  • D: Increasing exponential — ratios are 17/16 = 1.0625, 18/17 ≈ 1.0588, 19/18 ≈ 1.056; these are not constant, so it is not exponential. Eliminate D.
